proof_limits: compose_building assembles caller / COO declared node and edge choices only

axis_responsibility.Agent: No Agent Object, prompt, skill, tool policy, or hook is changed; this design Brick observes only and does not implement.
axis_responsibility.Brick: No Brick contract, write_scope, or work-statement surface is changed by the split.
axis_responsibility.Human: Success and quality verdicts, and the B2.4 retain-vs-remove decision and exact sub-profile cut, remain human/COO authority.
axis_responsibility.Link: Sufficiency and Movement remain the Link gate's; this design proposes no route/gate/movement.
axis_responsibility.Support: Owns checker profiles and their organization; this split is support-internal machinery reorganization (relocation of declarative pins/case sections across profile YAML files) and emits support evidence only.

candidate_file_changes: MODIFY (proposed): support/checkers/profiles/agent_axis_behavioral.yaml (remove F4 checks + their pins; keep self pin and F2/F3 behavioral)
MODIFY (proposed): support/checkers/profiles/core.yaml (path_allowlist.paths -- once in B2, once in B3)
MODIFY (proposed, optional/soft): support/checkers/module_registry.yaml pinned_by prose at lines ~704/1352 to track renamed/split profiles (documentation only, non-enforcing)
checker_or_verifier_plan: PYTHONPATH=.:support/import_identity python3 support/checkers/check_profile.py --self-test (registry closure: kernel_checks/rule-key subset, duplicate-key rejection).

Per new/edited profile: --profile <new B2 sub-profile A>, <B sub-profile>, <C sub-profile>, --profile provider_runtime_smoke, --profile agent_axis_behavioral (thinned), and --profile building_skill_preset_agent_tool_hardening only if retained.
--profile core to assert path_allowlist exactness after each core.yaml edit.

Mutation-RED probe per relocated check/section (temporarily break one moved pin or kernel check in its new home, confirm the new profile goes RED, then revert) to prove no silent-green from the move; do not leave any probe edit committed.
Optionally --all once the narrow profile runs pass and time remains (--all green remains support evidence only, not success/quality).
Assertion-conservation cross-check: diff the pre-split pin inventory against the post-split union to confirm superset (manual/grep evidence, recorded in observed_evidence).
returned.design_summary: Reorganize two oversized support-axis checker profiles by relocation only, preserving every existing assertion. B2: decompose building_skill_preset_agent_tool_hardening.yaml's ~21 case sections plus their supporting shape pins into 3 concern-coherent sub-profiles -- (1) builder/composition materialization, (2) agent-resource boundary (skill/tool/hook/retired-ref/preferred-adapter/candidate-packet), (3) preset-intake + adapter-gate + step-output/source-carry. B3: lift the F4 provider runtime-smoke kernel_checks {provider_preflight, gemini_api_adapter, design_ai_text_seams, codex_connect_stall_classification} together with their dedicated text pins out of agent_axis_behavioral.yaml into a new provider-runtime-smoke profile, leaving agent_axis as a thinned F2/F3 behavioral profile. Both steps add files, so both edit core.yaml path_allowlist; the shared core.yaml edit forces the B2-then-B3 serial order. No kernel runner or checker-lib code is changed: all moved kernel_check IDs already exist in KERNEL_DISPATCH and all section keys already exist in RULE_RUNNERS, so the moves are pure YAML relocation. The split is internal support reorganization that touches no Brick contract, Agent resource, or Link gate.
edge_cases: New profile file created but not added to core.yaml allowlist -> core profile RED (run_path_allowlist observed-minus-allowed). Both steps must edit core.yaml.

Original building_skill profile removed: run_path_allowlist tolerates a stale allowed entry (allowed-minus-observed is not raised), but core.yaml hygiene wants the entry dropped; also confirm no other profile path_exists references the removed file (verified: only module_registry/comment prose references it, which are non-enforcing).

Duplicate top-level section key inside one new sub-profile (e.g. if a section is accidentally listed twice) -> check_profile rejects duplicate same-key block (self-test lines 1086,1101); ensure each section appears in exactly one file.

Moving a kernel_check ID without its supporting text_contains pins, or vice versa, would split a check from its shape evidence; keep each moved F4 check together with its dedicated source-string pins so coverage is not orphaned in either file.

Shared baseline kernel_checks (package_path_admission, axis_contract_projection) needed by more than one sub-profile must be restated per sub-profile (factoring is deferred); restating is acceptable redundancy, dropping them is not.
profile_id collision across two new files is not caught by validate_profile; design requires distinct profile_ids to avoid ambiguous reporting.

A relocation that turns out to need a case_runners.py section-key or runner change (e.g. a section that hard-codes a single profile) -> stop and emit transition_concern_evidence rather than edit lib/*.

read_side 'and/or' optionality: pulling read_side provider checks in is optional; doing so adds a second self-pinned-file thinning and risks scope creep, so default-defer unless the runtime-smoke concern is incoherent without them.
invariants: Assertion conservation: the union of kernel_checks, rule blocks, and individual pins (each path_exists/path_absent/text_contains/text_absent/json_required_paths entry and each case-section item) across the post-split profiles must be a superset of the original set; no pin silently dropped or duplicated-away.
path_allowlist exactness: after each step, the rglob('*') file set under support/checkers/profiles must equal core.yaml path_allowlist.paths set (no on-disk file missing from the allowlist).

Registry closure (self-test): every new/edited profile's kernel_checks subset KERNEL_DISPATCH and rule keys subset RULE_RUNNERS; schema == checker-profile/v1; profile_id present and (design requirement) unique; no duplicate top-level section key within a single file.
No-new-runner: zero edits to support/checkers/lib/* and zero new KERNEL_DISPATCH IDs; a needed runner change becomes a transition_concern, not scope growth.
Serialization: B2's core.yaml edit must land before B3's core.yaml edit (shared chokepoint).
Self-pinned files persist: agent_axis_behavioral.yaml and read_side_projection_boundary.yaml remain on disk as thinned profiles.
Support-only return: no success/quality/Movement claim is produced by the implementation.
not_proven: That the proposed 3-way B2 partition is the only valid concern cut (it is a candidate; the exact section->sub-profile mapping and 2-vs-3 sub-profile count are a caller/COO decision).
Whether building_skill_preset_agent_tool_hardening.yaml should be removed or retained as a thinned shell (B2.4 decision not pinned by the task).

Whether any case section hard-codes a single profile name in support/checkers/lib/* and therefore needs a lib edit to relocate cleanly (not inspected line-by-line; would trigger a transition_concern).

That core.yaml path_allowlist is the ONLY file requiring edit for the new profiles (other profile path_exists pins or registry prose may also reference profile paths; verified only that no enforcing path pin targets the split files besides the self-pins).
Whether the four F4 checks' supporting text pins are fully enumerated for clean co-relocation (the exact pin set must be inventoried during implementation).
Post-split assertion completeness and mutation-RED behavior of each relocated check (must be executed in the implementation Building; this design Brick ran no checker).
Implementation readiness and semantic fitness of the proposed organization.
Source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, and Movement authority -- all outside this design Brick.
observed_evidence: Task source (work/task.md of checker-profile-diet-impl-0625): mandatory internal order B2 then B3; B2 splits support/checkers/profiles/building_skill_preset_agent_tool_hardening.yaml into concern-coherent sub-profiles preserving all assertions; B3 separates provider runtime-smoke checks out of agent_axis_behavioral.yaml (and/or read_side_projection_boundary.yaml) into a dedicated runtime-smoke profile; step 3 update core.yaml path_allowlist; step 4 do not edit support/checkers/lib/* unless absolutely necessary (else return transition_concern_evidence).

Prior plan Brick return (buildings/checker-profile-diet-implementation-plan-0625/.../plan-attempt-1/step-output.json): names the same two-step serial scope, the B2 concern partition (a builder/composition, b agent-resource boundary, c preset-intake+adapter-gate), the B3 F4 set {provider_preflight, gemini_api_adapter, design_ai_text_seams, codex_connect_stall_classification}, and the constraint that core.yaml path_allowlist is an EXACT rglob set forcing B2-before-B3 serialization.

support/checkers/lib/rule_runners.py run_path_allowlist (lines 74-92): observed = every file under support/checkers/profiles via root.rglob('*'); raises ProfileError on sorted(observed - allowed). A stale allowed entry (allowed - observed) does NOT raise; only an unlisted on-disk file does.

support/checkers/profiles/core.yaml lines 68-89: path_allowlist root support/checkers/profiles enumerating the 24 current profile files including building_skill_preset_agent_tool_hardening.yaml (line 79) and core.yaml.

support/checkers/check_profile.py assert_registry_closure (lines 856-882) + validate_profile (892-905): each profile's kernel_checks must be subset of KERNEL_DISPATCH, rule keys subset of RULE_RUNNERS, schema must equal checker-profile/v1, profile_id required, unknown top-level keys rejected; self-test also rejects duplicate same-key top-level blocks and duplicate mapping keys (lines 1086,1101).

support/checkers/profiles/building_skill_preset_agent_tool_hardening.yaml top-level keys (grepped): kernel_checks {package_path_admission, axis_contract_projection, agent_adapter_return_shape}; shape rules path_exists(8), path_absent(43), text_contains(47), json_required_paths(227), text_absent(243); case sections at lines 257 materialize_building_intent_case, 1134 _rejects, 1361 preset_building_completion_case, 1426 building_intake_seam_case, 1532 intake_project_vessel_case, 1555 run_once_task_source_admission_case, 1571 adapter_gate_shape_union_case, 1579 source_fact_body_carry_case, 1744 wiki_carry_truncation_survival_case, 1747 step_output_drain_case, 1791 _rejects, 1805 gate_sequence_policy_case, 1835 compose_building_case, 2121 compose_building_rejects (largest block to ~3427), 3427 agent_resource_boundary, 3592 agent_resource_retired_ref_rejects, 3605 agent_preferred_adapter_rejects, 3660 declared_step_template_plan_case, 3867 _rejects, 4103 agent_candidate_packet_case, 4147 preset_ranking_packet_case; proof_limits 4162; not_proven 4168.

support/checkers/profiles/agent_axis_behavioral.yaml: kernel_checks lines 4-57 include the F4 runtime-smoke set provider_preflight(9), gemini_api_adapter(22), design_ai_text_seams(27), codex_connect_stall_classification(38), plus F2/F3 behavioral checks (package_path_admission, axis_contract_projection, building_lifecycle_path_shape, building_map_graph, agent_adapter_return_shape, agent_session_id_redaction, casting_node_carry); self path_exists pin at line 59; supporting text_contains pins for the moved checks reference adapter_subprocess.preflight_provider, gemini strings, codex stall strings, design_ai_text_seams.

support/checkers/profiles/read_side_projection_boundary.yaml: self path_exists pin at line 21; hosts F6 read-side projection kernel_checks plus some live/provider checks (mcp_stdio_smoke, connect_config_launch, codex/claude_projection_native, install_script_lint, release_export_exclusion, product_no_smith_residue).

support/checkers/module_registry.yaml references the profile names only as prose pinned_by comments (lines 704, 1352) and read_side json pins prose (kernel_checks.py:4100); these are documentation strings, not enforced path pins.

support/docs/references/checker-profile-map.md (HEAD 4c1958a): F4 runtime-smoke family (lines 103-112), section 2 four-way shared baseline overlap (package_path_admission x9, building_lifecycle_path_shape x8, building_map_graph x8, axis_contract_projection x7) deferred not factored, section 5 chokepoints (core.yaml, module_registry.yaml, lib/case_runners.py, lib/kernel_checks.py, lib/rule_runners.py), section 6 B2-before-B3 serialization proposal.
returned.output_excerpt: I have grounded the design across the plan, the reference map, both target profiles, core.yaml's allowlist mechanism, and the runner self-test. Here is the design contract. ```json { "observed_evidence": [ "Task source (work/task.md of checker-profile-diet-impl-0625): mandatory internal order B2 then B3; B2 splits support/checkers/profiles/building_skill_preset_agent_tool_hardening.yaml into concern-coherent sub-profiles preserving all assertions; B3 separates provider runtime-smoke checks out of agent_axis_behavioral.yaml (and/or read_side_projection_boundary.yaml) into a dedicated runtime...
proposed_changes: B2.1 (proposed): create concern sub-profile A (builder/composition materialization) carrying the materialize_building_intent_case, materialize_building_intent_rejects, compose_building_case, compose_building_rejects, declared_step_template_plan_case, declared_step_template_plan_rejects, gate_sequence_policy_case sections plus the text_contains/text_absent/json_required_paths pins that assert builder/composition/materializer surfaces, and the kernel_checks the composition cases depend on (package_path_admission, axis_contract_projection).

B2.2 (proposed): create concern sub-profile B (agent-resource boundary hardening) carrying agent_resource_boundary, agent_resource_retired_ref_rejects, agent_preferred_adapter_rejects, agent_candidate_packet_case sections plus the agent_resources.py / tool_policies / hook / retired-ref text pins and kernel_check agent_adapter_return_shape.

B2.3 (proposed): create concern sub-profile C (preset-intake + adapter-gate) carrying preset_building_completion_case, preset_ranking_packet_case, building_intake_seam_case, intake_project_vessel_case, run_once_task_source_admission_case, adapter_gate_shape_union_case, source_fact_body_carry_case, wiki_carry_truncation_survival_case, step_output_drain_case, step_output_drain_rejects plus their preset/intake text pins.

B2.4 (proposed, caller/COO decision): either delete the original building_skill_preset_agent_tool_hardening.yaml once every section lands in A/B/C, or retain it as a thinned shell. Each new file carries its own schema, unique profile_id, proof_limits, not_proven.
B2.5 (proposed): update core.yaml path_allowlist.paths to add the new sub-profile files and (if the original is removed) drop its entry; this is the first of the two serialized core.yaml edits.

B3.1 (proposed): create support/checkers/profiles/provider_runtime_smoke.yaml with kernel_checks {provider_preflight, gemini_api_adapter, design_ai_text_seams, codex_connect_stall_classification} (IDs already in KERNEL_DISPATCH) plus the text_contains pins that specifically assert those moved checks' source surfaces (adapter_subprocess.preflight_provider, gemini API strings, codex connect-stall strings, design_ai_text_seams strings), carrying schema/profile_id/proof_limits/not_proven.

B3.2 (proposed): remove those four kernel_check IDs and their dedicated text pins from agent_axis_behavioral.yaml, leaving the F2/F3 behavioral checks, the capability/write-scope/model/hook case sections, and the self path_exists pin (line 59) intact so the file remains on disk as a thinned profile.
B3.3 (proposed): update core.yaml path_allowlist.paths to add provider_runtime_smoke.yaml; this is the second serialized core.yaml edit, mandatorily after B2.

Optional (proposed, defer unless coherent): re-home read_side_projection_boundary.yaml provider/live checks into the new runtime-smoke profile; task says 'and/or', so default is to leave read_side untouched.

relevant_current_structure.allowlist_gate: support/checkers/profiles/core.yaml path_allowlist (lines 68-89) is an exact rglob set over support/checkers/profiles enforced by rule_runners.run_path_allowlist; any new profile file absent from this set turns the core profile RED.
relevant_current_structure.b2_target: support/checkers/profiles/building_skill_preset_agent_tool_hardening.yaml (4172 lines): kernel_checks=[package_path_admission, axis_contract_projection, agent_adapter_return_shape]; shape rule blocks path_exists/path_absent/text_contains/json_required_paths/text_absent; ~21 case sections (materialize_building_intent_case/_rejects, compose_building_case/_rejects, declared_step_template_plan_case/_rejects, gate_sequence_policy_case, step_output_drain_case/_rejects, source_fact_body_carry_case, wiki_carry_truncation_survival_case, preset_building_completion_case, building_intake_seam_case, intake_project_vessel_case, run_once_task_source_admission_case, adapter_gate_shape_union_case, agent_resource_boundary, agent_resource_retired_ref_rejects, agent_preferred_adapter_rejects, agent_candidate_packet_case, preset_ranking_packet_case); proof_limits + not_proven trailers.
relevant_current_structure.b3_target: support/checkers/profiles/agent_axis_behavioral.yaml: mixes F4 runtime-smoke kernel_checks (provider_preflight, gemini_api_adapter, design_ai_text_seams, codex_connect_stall_classification) with F2/F3 behavioral checks and capability/write-scope/model/hook case sections; self path_exists pin at line 59.
relevant_current_structure.runner_registry: support/checkers/check_profile.py KERNEL_DISPATCH/RULE_RUNNERS + assert_registry_closure self-test; all IDs needed by the moves already registered.

returned.returned_summary: local CLI Agent Adapter returned support evidence
unchanged_surfaces: support/checkers/lib/case_runners.py, kernel_checks.py, rule_runners.py (no runner code change; if any relocation needs a lib edit, return transition_concern_evidence instead of broadening scope).
KERNEL_DISPATCH and RULE_RUNNERS registries in check_profile.py (no new kernel-check or rule-key registration; all moved IDs/section keys pre-exist).
Every existing assertion's semantics: pins are relocated, not weakened, deleted, or rewritten.
agent_axis_behavioral.yaml file presence (self path_exists pin line 59) and read_side_projection_boundary.yaml file presence (self pin line 21) -- both stay on disk.
Brick contracts, Agent resources, Link gates/movements, presets, and all non-checker repository surfaces.
The four-way shared baseline preamble (map section 2) -- factoring deferred to a separate admission Building.

subject: checker-profile-diet-impl-0625-design
